dtcs.description: Driver's MICU Internal (EEPROM) Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B10A2 indicated? Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box, USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (USA Models), Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (Canada Models). Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

dtcs.description: Left Daytime Running Lights Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Release the parking brake lever.
Turn the headlight switch OFF.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). Is DTC B10CF indicated? Go to step 6. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the headlight high beam ON. Does the left headlight (high beam) come on? Go to step 7. Go to step 9.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Measure the voltage between body ground and driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector Q (20P)   terminals No. 1   and No. 2, and between body ground and driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector R (24P) terminals No. 2   and No. 5   individually. Is there 0.2 V or less? Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box, USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (USA Models), Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (Canada Models). Repair an open or high resistance in the ground wire(s) or poor ground (G501,G502).
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Turn the headlight switch OFF.

Check the No. 28 (10 A) fuse in the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK? Go to step 12. Replace the fuse. If the fuse is blows again, repair a short to ground on the No. 28 (10 A)   fuse circuit.
Check the left headlight bulb. Is the bulb OK? Go to step 13. Replace the bulb and recheck.
Disconnect driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (33P).
Disconnect the left headlight (high beam) 2P connector.

Check for continuity between driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (33P) terminal No. 13   and left headlight (high beam) 2P connector terminal No. 2. Is there continuity? Go to step 16. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Check for continuity between left headlight (high beam) 2P connector terminal No. 1   and body ground. Is there continuity? Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box, USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (USA Models), Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (Canada Models). Repair an open or high resistance in the ground wire or poor ground (G302).

dtcs.description: Passenger's MICU Internal (EEPROM) Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B11A2 indicated? Faulty passenger's MICU; replace the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Passenger's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Passenger's MICU) Removal and Installation. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.

dtcs.description: Right Daytime Running Lights Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Release the parking brake lever.
Turn the headlight switch OFF.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). Is DTC B11CF indicated? Go to step 6. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the headlight high beam ON. Does the right headlight (high beam) come on? Go to step 7. Go to step 9.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Measure the voltage between body ground and passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box connector A (38P)   terminals No. 36   and No. 37, and between body ground and passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box connector E (18P) terminals No. 15   and No. 16   individually. Is there 0.2 V or less? Faulty passenger's MICU; replace the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Passenger's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Passenger's MICU) Removal and Installation. Repair an open or high resistance in the ground wire(s) or poor ground (G201,G505).
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Turn the headlight switch OFF.

Check the No. 1 (10 A) fuse in the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK? Go to step 12. Replace the fuse. If the fuse blows again, repair a short to ground on the No. 1 (10 A) fuse circuit.
Check the right headlight bulb. Is the bulb OK? Go to step 13. Replace the bulb and recheck.
Disconnect passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box connector E (18P).
Disconnect the right headlight (high beam) 2P connector.

Check for continuity between passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box connector E (18P) terminal No. 2   and right headlight (high beam) 2P connector terminal No. 2. Is there continuity? Go to step 16. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Check for continuity between right headlight (high beam) 2P connector terminal No. 1   and body ground. Is there continuity? Faulty passenger's MICU; replace the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Passenger's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Passenger's MICU) Removal and Installation. Repair an open or high resistance in the ground wire or poor ground (G201).

dtcs.description: Passenger's MICU IG1 Line Input Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B11C7 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections. If the connections are good, check the battery condition  See: Starting and Charging/Battery/Testing and Inspection/Battery Test   and the charging system.
Check the DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B11C7 indicated with DTC U1282? Go to DTC U1282 troubleshooting  See: U Code Charts/U1282. Go to step 6.

Measure the voltage between passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box connector A (38P) terminal No. 35   and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Faulty passenger's MICU; replace the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Passenger's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Passenger's MICU) Removal and Installation. Repair an open in the wire between the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box and the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box.

dtcs.description: or DTC indicator G and A/C ON: An Open in the Mode Control Motor Circuit

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Do the Self-Diagnostic Function with the HDS   or the climate control unit.
Check for DTCs. Is DTC B121A or G and A/C ON indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connections on the mode control motor circuit.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Test the mode control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Testing and Inspection/Mode Control Motor Test. Is the mode control motor OK? Go to step 7. Replace the mode control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Service and Repair/Mode Control Motor Replacement.
Disconnect the mode control motor 7P connector.
Disconnect climate control unit connectors A (28P) and B (12P).

Check for continuity between the following terminals of climate control unit connectors A (28P), B (12P), and the mode control motor 7P connector. Is there continuity? Check for loose wires or poor connections at climate control unit   connectors A (28P), B (12P), and at the mode control motor   7P connector. If the connections are good, substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation. Repair an open in the wire(s) between the climate control unit   and the mode control motor.

dtcs.description: or DTC indicator H and A/C ON: A Short in the Mode Control Motor Circuit

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Do the Self-Diagnostic Function with the HDS   or the climate control unit.
Check for DTCs. Is DTC B121B or H and A/C ON indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connection on the mode control motor circuit.
Check for DTCs. Are these DTCs also present; B1220 or L and A/C ON, and/or B1234 or B and A/C ON, and/or B1237 or E and A/C ON? Go to step 13. Go to step 6.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Test the mode control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Testing and Inspection/Mode Control Motor Test. Is the mode control motor OK? Go to step 8. Replace the mode control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Service and Repair/Mode Control Motor Replacement.
Disconnect the mode control motor 7P connector.
Disconnect climate control unit connectors A (28P) and B (12P).

Check for continuity between climate control unit connector A (28P) terminal No. 11   and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to body ground in the wire between the climate control unit   and the mode control motor. Go to step 11.

Check for continuity between climate control unit connector A (28P) terminal No. 11   and climate control unit connector B (12P) terminal No. 12. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wires between the climate control unit   and the mode control motor. Go to step 12.

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and measure the voltage between climate control unit connector A (28P) terminal No. 11   and body ground. Is there any voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire between the climate control unit   and the mode control motor. This short may also damage the climate control unit. Repair a short to power before replacing the climate control unit. Substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and disconnect climate control unit connector B (12P).
Disconnect these items: - Driver's air mix control motor - Passenger's air mix control motor - Recirculation control motor - Mode control motor

Check for continuity between climate control unit connector B (12P) terminal No. 12   and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to body ground in the wire between the climate control unit   and the mode control motor. Go to step 16.

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and measure the voltage between climate control unit connector B (12P) terminal No. 12 and body ground. Is there any voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire between the climate control unit   and the mode control motor. This short may have also damaged the climate control unit. Repair short to power before replacing the climate control unit. Go to step 17.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Reconnect climate control unit connector B (12P).

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and measure the voltage between climate control unit connector B (12P) terminal No. 12   and body ground. Is there about 5 V? Go to step 20. Check for loose wires or poor connections at climate control unit   connector B (12P). If the connection is good, substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation.

While measuring the voltage between climate control unit connector B (12P) terminal No. 12 and body ground, reconnect these items individually and note the voltage reading each time: - Driver's air mix control motor - Passenger's air mix control motor - Recirculation control motor - Mode control motor Does the voltage remain at about 5 V? Substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation. Replace the component that caused the voltage drop.

dtcs.description: Driver's MICU IG1 Line Input Error

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1036 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections at the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box   connector C (5P). If the connections are good, check the battery condition  See: Starting and Charging/Battery/Testing and Inspection/Battery Test   and the charging system.

Measure the voltage between gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 15   and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box, USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (USA Models), Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (Canada Models). Repair an open in the wire between the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box   and the gauge control module.

dtcs.description: Wiper Auto-Stop (As) Signal Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Turn the wiper switch to LOW or HIGH for at least 15 seconds, then turn the wiper switch OFF.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1077 indicated? Check for loose or poor connections at the driver's MICU and the wiper motor. If the connections are OK, replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box, USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (USA Models), Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (Canada Models). Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Do the windshield wiper motor test  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Wiper Motor/Testing and Inspection. Is the windshield wiper motor OK? Go to step 7. Replace the wiper motor  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Testing and Inspection/Wiper Motor Test, and recheck.
Disconnect the windshield wiper motor 4P connector.
Disconnect driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (33P).

Check for continuity between windshield wiper motor 4P connector terminal No. 3 and driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (33P) terminal No. 9. Is there continuity? Go to step 10. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.
Check for continuity between windshield wiper motor 4P   connector terminal No. 3   and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 11.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between windshield wiper motor 4P connector terminal No. 3   and body ground. Is there voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire. Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box, USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (USA Models), Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (Canada Models).
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Measure the voltage between under-hood fuse/relay box connector C (5P) terminal No. 2   and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 16. Go to step 15.

Check the No. 3-7   (WIP MTR) (30 A)   fuse in the under-hood fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK? Repair an open or high resistance in the No. 3-7   (WIP MTR) (30 A)   fuse circuit. Replace the fuse. If the fuse blows again, repair a short to ground on the No. 3-7   (WIP MTR) (30 A)   fuse circuit.

Do the relay circuit board test  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Relay/Testing and Inspection. Is the relay circuit board OK? Go to step 17. Replace the relay circuit board, and recheck.
Disconnect under-hood fuse/relay box connector B (14P).

Check for continuity between under-hood fuse/relay box connector B (14P) terminal No. 4   and body ground. Is there continuity? Go to step 19. Repair an open or high resistance in the ground wire or poor ground (G301).

Do the windshield wiper motor test  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Wiper Motor/Testing and Inspection. Is the windshield wiper motor OK? Go to step 20. Replace the windshield wiper motor  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Testing and Inspection/Wiper Motor Test, and recheck.
Reconnect under-hood fuse/relay box connector B (14P).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between body ground and windshield wiper motor 4P connector terminal No. 2   with the wiper switch ON (Low), and measure the voltage between body ground and windshield wiper motor 4P connector terminal No. 4   with the wiper switch ON (High) individually. Is there battery voltage? Repair an open or high resistance in the wire(s) between the windshield wiper motor   and body ground. If the wire(s) is OK, check for poor ground (G302). Go to step 23.

Measure the voltage between body ground and under-hood fuse/relay box connector B (14P) terminal No. 5   with the wiper switch ON (Low), and measure the voltage between body ground and under-hood fuse/relay box connector B (14P) terminal No. 6   with the wiper switch ON (High) individually. Is there battery voltage? Repair an open or high resistance in the BLU (LO) or YEL (HI) wire(s). Check terminals F7   and F8 of the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector F (33P) using the input test  See: Wiper and Washer Systems/Testing and Inspection/Wipers/Washers (Driver's and Passenger's MICU) Input Test. If the input tests prove OK, replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box, USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (USA Models), Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (Canada Models).

dtcs.description: , B1126 DTC B1125: Driver's Power Window Motor A-Pulse Malfunction DTC B1126: Driver's Power Window Motor B-Pulse Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Open and close the driver's power window by using the power window master switch manually.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Are DTCs B1125 and/or B1126 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections between the door multiplex control unit and the driver's power window motor.
Select the POWER WINDOWS from the BODY ELECTRICAL SYSTEM SELECT menu, and enter the DATA LIST.

Check the DETECT/NONE information of the DRIVER'S P/W MOTOR PULSE A and PULSE B signal in the DATA LIST. Does the information indicator display DETECT while the window is moving, and display NONE when the window is stopped? Replace the power window master switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch Replacement. Go to step 7.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the power window master switch 37P connector.
Disconnect the driver's power window motor 6P connector.

Check for continuity between power window master switch 37P connector terminals No. 3,No. 20,   and No. 22 and driver's power window motor 6P connector terminals No. 5,No. 3,   and No. 6   respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 11. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire(s) between the door multiplex control unit and the driver's power window motor.

Check for continuity between driver's power window motor 6P connector terminals No. 3, No. 5,   and No. 6   and body ground individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire(s). Go to step 12.

Test the driver's power window motor hall IC (pulsar) test. Is the motor OK? Replace the power window master switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch Replacement. Faulty driver's power window motor; replace the driver's door regulator  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Window Glass/Service and Repair.

dtcs.description: B1125, B1126 DTC B1125: Driver's Power Window Motor A-Pulse Malfunction DTC B1126: Driver's Power Window Motor B-Pulse Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Open and close the driver's power window by using the power window master switch manually.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Are DTCs B1125 and/or B1126 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections between the door multiplex control unit and the driver's power window motor.
Select the POWER WINDOWS from the BODY ELECTRICAL SYSTEM SELECT menu, and enter the DATA LIST.

Check the DETECT/NONE information of the DRIVER'S P/W MOTOR PULSE A and PULSE B signal in the DATA LIST. Does the information indicator display DETECT while the window is moving, and display NONE when the window is stopped? Replace the power window master switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch Replacement. Go to step 7.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the power window master switch 37P connector.
Disconnect the driver's power window motor 6P connector.

Check for continuity between power window master switch 37P connector terminals No. 3,No. 20,   and No. 22 and driver's power window motor 6P connector terminals No. 5,No. 3,   and No. 6   respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 11. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire(s) between the door multiplex control unit and the driver's power window motor.

Check for continuity between driver's power window motor 6P connector terminals No. 3, No. 5,   and No. 6   and body ground individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire(s). Go to step 12.

Test the driver's power window motor hall IC (pulsar) test. Is the motor OK? Replace the power window master switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch Replacement. Faulty driver's power window motor; replace the driver's door regulator  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Window Glass/Service and Repair.

dtcs.description: Driver's Door Key Cylinder Switch Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
With one ignition key, turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Insert the ignition key into the driver's door key cylinder switch, and turn the key to the LOCK and UNLOCK positions 10 times.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1127 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the driver's door key cylinder switch   system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connection.
With the driver's door key cylinder in the neutral position, select KEYLESS TRANSMITTER with the HDS, and enter the DATA LIST.

Check the ON/OFF information of the DRIVER'S DOOR KEY CYLINDER SWITCH (LOCK) and DRIVER'S DOOR KEY CYLINDER SWITCH (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Are both information indicators OFF? Go to step 12. Go to step 7.
Disconnect the driver's door lock actuator 10P connector.

Check the ON/OFF information of the DRIVER'S DOOR KEY CYLINDER SWITCH (LOCK) and DRIVER'S DOOR KEY CYLINDER SWITCH (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Are both information indicators OFF? Faulty driver's door key cylinder switch; replace the driver's door latch  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Latch/Service and Repair. Go to step 9.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the power window master switch 37P connector.

Check for continuity between body ground and power window master switch 37P connector terminals No. 28   and No. 29   individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the LOCK or UNLOCK wire(s). Replace the power window master switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch Replacement.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the driver's door lock actuator 10P connector.
Disconnect the power window master switch 37P connector.

Check for continuity between power window master switch 37P connector terminals No. 28   and No. 29. Is there continuity? Repair a short between the LOCK and UNLOCK wires. Substitute a known-good power window master switch. If the symptom goes away, replace the original power window master switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch Replacement. If not, the driver's door key cylinder switch   is faulty; replace the driver's door latch  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Latch/Service and Repair.

dtcs.description: Driver's Door Lock Switch Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Lock and unlock the driver's door with the driver's door lock switch.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1128 indicated? Replace the power window master switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch Replacement. Intermittent failure, the driver's door lock switch system is OK at this time. Check for a loose or poor connection.

dtcs.description: Driver's Door Lock Knob Switch Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Operate the driver's door lock knob switch several times.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1129 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the driver's door lock knob switch   system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Select KEYLESS TRANSMITTER from the BODY ELECTRICAL SYSTEM SELECT menu, and enter the DATA LIST.

Check the ON/OFF information of the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (LOCK) and the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (UNLOCK). Is the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (LOCK) information indicator ON and the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (UNLOCK) information indicator OFF with the driver's door lock knob switch in LOCK position, and is the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (LOCK) information indicator OFF and the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (UNLOCK) information indicator ON with the driver's door lock knob switch in UNLOCK position? Replace the power window master switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch Replacement. Go to step 7.
Disconnect the driver's door lock actuator 10P connector.

Check the ON/OFF information of the DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (LOCK) and DRIVER'S DOOR LOCK KNOB SWITCH (UNLOCK) in the DATA LIST. Are both information indicators OFF? Go to step 12. Go to step 9.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the power window master switch 37P connector.

Check for continuity between body ground and power window master switch 37P connector terminals No. 30   and No. 31   individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the LOCK or UNLOCK wire(s). Replace the power window master switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch Replacement.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the power window master switch 37P connector.

Check for continuity between power window master switch 37P connector terminals No. 30   and No. 31. Is there continuity? Repair a short between the LOCK and UNLOCK wires. Check for an open or high resistance in the driver's door lock switch (LOCK) wire or the driver's door lock knob switch   (UNLOCK) wire between the door multiplex control unit and the driver's door lock knob switch. If the wires are OK, the driver's door lock knob switch is faulty; replace the driver's door latch  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Latch/Service and Repair.

dtcs.description: , B1131 DTC B1130: Front Passenger's Power Window Motor A-Pulse Malfunction DTC B1131: Front Passenger's Power Window Motor B-Pulse Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Open and close the front passenger's power window by using the front passenger's power window switch manually.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Are DTCs B1130 and/or B1131 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections between the front passenger's power window switch and the front passenger's power window motor.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the front passenger's power window switch 37P connector.
Disconnect the front passenger's power window motor 6P connector.

Check for continuity between front passenger's power window switch 37P connector terminals No. 26,No. 27,   and No. 28 and the front passenger's power window motor 6P connector terminals No. 5,No. 6,   and No. 3   respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 9. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire(s) between the front passenger's window switch and the front passenger's power window motor.

Check for continuity between front passenger's power window motor 6P   connector terminals No. 3,No. 5,   and No. 6   and body ground individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire(s). Go to step 10.

Test the front passenger's power window motor hall IC (pulsar) test. Is the motor OK? Replace the front passenger's power window switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Front Passenger's Power Window Switch Replacement. Faulty front passenger's power window motor; replace the front passenger's door regulator  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Window Glass/Service and Repair.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1125 or B1126 indicated? Troubleshoot the DTC B1125 or B1126  See: B1125. Go to step 5.
Reset the power window control unit  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Windows and Glass/Power Window Control Module/Testing and Inspection.
Check for DTCs again with the HDS. Is DTC B1140 indicated? Go to step 7. The system has recovered at this time.
Substitute a known-good power window master switch.
Open and close the driver's power window by using the driver's switch manually.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1140 indicated? Faulty driver's power window motor; replace the driver's door regulator  See: Body and Frame/Doors, Hood and Trunk/Doors/Front Door/Front Door Window Glass/Service and Repair. dtcs.description: Door Multiplex Control Unit Lost Communication With Other Units (UART communication error)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Open and close all power windows by using the power window master switch.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1142 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect these items. - Power window master switch 37P connector - Front passenger's power window switch 37P connector - Moonroof control unit/motor 14P connector

Check for continuity between power window master switch 37P connector terminal No. 27 and front passenger's power window switch 37P connector terminal No. 29. Is there continuity? Go to step 8. Repair an open or high resistance in the UART wire.

Check for continuity between power window master switch 37P connector terminal No. 27   and moonroof control unit/motor 14P connector terminal No. 11. Is there continuity? Go to step 9. Repair an open or high resistance in the UART wire.
Check for continuity between power window master switch 37P connector terminal No. 27   and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the UART wire. Go to step 10.
Reconnect the power window master switch 37P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between power window master switch 37P connector terminal No. 27   and body ground. Is there less than 0.2 V? Go to the front passenger's power window switch input test, and do all the power and ground input tests  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Testing and Inspection/Front Passenger's Power Window Switch Input Test. If the tests prove OK, substitute a known good front passenger's power window switch. If the DTC goes away, replace the original front passenger's power window switch See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Front Passenger's Power Window Switch Replacement. If the DTC is still present, replace the power window master switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch Replacement. Repair a short to power in the UART wire.

dtcs.description: Fuel Level Sensor (Fuel Gauge Sending Unit) Circuit Open

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTCs B1175 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the fuel tank unit 4P connector.
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.

Check for continuity between gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 6   and No. 18   and fuel tank unit 4P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 3   respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 9. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire(s).

Do the fuel gauge sending unit test  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Tank Unit/Testing and Inspection. Is the fuel gauge sending unit OK? Replace the gauge control module  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair. Faulty fuel gauge sending unit; replace the fuel tank unit  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Pump/Service and Repair/Fuel Tank Unit Removal/Installation.

dtcs.description: Fuel Level Sensor (Fuel Gauge Sending Unit) Circuit Short

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for at least 30 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1176 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the fuel level sensor circuit is OK at this time. Check for worn/missing insulation or an internal short in the wire.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the fuel tank unit 4P connector.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Wait for at least 30 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1176 indicated? Go to step 11. Faulty fuel gauge sending unit; replace the fuel tank unit  See: Powertrain Management/Fuel Delivery and Air Induction/Fuel Pump/Service and Repair/Fuel Pump/Fuel Gauge Sending Unit Replacement.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.

Check for continuity between gauge control module 32P connector terminal No. 6   and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire between the gauge control module   and the fuel tank unit. Go to step 14.

Check for continuity between gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 6   and No. 18. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wires between the gauge control module   and the fuel tank unit. Replace the gauge control module  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair.

dtcs.description: or DTC indicator L and A/C ON: A Short in the Recirculation Control Motor Circuit

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Do the Self-Diagnostic Function with the HDS   or the climate control unit.
Check for DTCs. Is DTC B1220 or L and A/C ON indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connection on the recirculation control motor circuit.
Check for DTCs. Are these DTCs also present; B121B or H and A/C ON, and/or B1234 or B and A/C ON, and/or B1237 or E and A/C ON? Go to step 13. Go to step 6.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Test the recirculation control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Testing and Inspection/Recirculation Control Motor Test. Is the recirculation control motor OK? Go to step 8. Replace the recirculation control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Service and Repair/Recirculation Control Motor Replacement.
Disconnect the recirculation control motor 7P connector.
Disconnect climate control unit connectors A (28P) and B (12P).

Check for continuity between climate control unit connector A (28P) terminal No. 12   and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to body ground in the wire between the climate control unit   and the recirculation control motor. Go to step 11.

Check for continuity between climate control unit connector A (28P) terminal No. 12   and climate control unit connector B (12P) terminal No. 12. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wires between the climate control unit   and the recirculation control motor. Go to step 12.

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and measure the voltage between climate control unit connector A (28P) terminal No. 12   and body ground. Is there any voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire between the climate control unit   and the recirculation control motor. This short may also damage the climate control unit. Repair a short to power before replacing the climate control unit. Substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and disconnect climate control unit connector B (12P).
Disconnect these items: - Driver's air mix control motor - Passenger's air mix control motor - Recirculation control motor - Mode control motor

Check for continuity between climate control unit connector B (12P) terminal No. 12   and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to body ground in the wire between the climate control unit   and the recirculation control motor. Go to step 16.

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and measure the voltage between climate control unit connector B (12P)   terminal No. 12   and body ground. Is there any voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire between the climate control unit   and the recirculation control motor. This short may have also damaged the climate control unit. Repair a short to power before replacing the climate control unit. Go to step 17.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Reconnect climate control unit connector B (12P).

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and measure the voltage between climate control unit connector B (12P) terminal No. 12   and body ground. Is there about 5 V? Go to step 20. Check for loose wires or poor connections at climate control unit   connector B (12P). If the connection is good, substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation.

While measuring the voltage between climate control unit connector B (12P)   terminal No. 12   and body ground, reconnect these items individually and note the voltage reading each time: - Driver's air mix control motor - Passenger's air mix control motor - Recirculation control motor - Mode control motor Does the voltage remain at about 5 V? Substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation. Replace the component that caused the voltage drop.

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Do the Self-Diagnostic Function with the HDS   or the climate control unit.
Check for DTCs. Is DTC B1233 or A and A/C ON indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connections on the driver's air mix control motor circuit.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Test the driver's air mix control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Testing and Inspection/Driver's Air Mix Control Motor Test. Is the driver's air mix control motor OK? Go to step 7. Replace the driver's air mix control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Service and Repair/Driver's Air Mix Control Motor Replacement.
Disconnect the driver's air mix control motor 7P connector.
Disconnect climate control unit connectors A (28P) and B (12P).

Check for continuity between the following terminals of climate control unit connectors A (28P), B (12P), and the driver's air mix control motor 7P connector. Is there continuity? Check for loose wires or poor connections at climate control unit connectors A (28P), B (12P), and at the driver's air mix control motor   7P connector. If the connections are good, substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation. Repair an open in the wire(s) between the climate control unit and the driver's air mix control motor.

dtcs.description: or DTC indicator B and A/C ON: A Short in the Air Mix Control Motor Circuit (Driver's)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Do the Self-Diagnostic Function with the HDS   or the climate control unit.
Check for DTCs. Is DTC B1234 or B and A/C ON indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connections on the driver's air mix control motor circuit.
Check for DTCs. Are these DTCs also present; B121B or H and A/C ON, and/or B1220 or L and A/C ON, and/or B1237 or E and A/C ON? Go to step 13. Go to step 6.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Test the driver's air mix control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Testing and Inspection/Driver's Air Mix Control Motor Test. Is the driver's air mix control motor OK? Go to step 8. Replace the driver's air mix control motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Air Door/Air Door Actuator / Motor/Service and Repair/Driver's Air Mix Control Motor Replacement.
Disconnect the driver's air mix control motor 7P connector.
Disconnect climate control unit connectors A (28P) and B (12P).

Check for continuity between climate control unit connector A (28P) terminal No. 14   and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to body ground in the wire between the climate control unit and the driver's air mix control motor. Go to step 11.

Check for continuity between climate control unit connector A (28P) terminal No. 14   and climate control unit connector B (12P) terminal No. 12. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wires between the climate control unit and the driver's air mix control motor. Go to step 12.

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and measure the voltage between climate control unit connector A (28P) terminal No. 14   and body ground. Is there any voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire between the climate control unit and the driver's air mix control motor. This short may also damage the climate control unit. Repair a short to power before replacing the climate control unit. Substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and disconnect climate control unit connector B (12P).
Disconnect these items: - Driver's air mix control motor - Passenger's air mix control motor - Recirculation control motor - Mode control motor

Check for continuity between climate control unit connector B (12P) terminal No. 12   and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short to body ground in the wire between the climate control unit and the driver's air mix control motor. Go to step 16.

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and measure the voltage between climate control unit connector B (12P)   terminal No. 12   and body ground. Is there any voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire between the climate control unit and the driver's air mix control motor. This short may have also damaged the climate control unit. Repair a short to power before replacing the climate control unit. Go to step 17.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Reconnect climate control unit connector B (12P).

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and measure the voltage between climate control unit connector B (12P) terminal No. 12   and body ground. Is there about 5 V? Go to step 20. Check for loose wires or poor connections at climate control unit   connector B (12P). If the connection is good, substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation.

While measuring the voltage between climate control unit connector B (12P)   terminal No. 12 and body ground, reconnect these items individually and note the voltage reading each time: - Driver's air mix control motor - Passenger's air mix control motor - Recirculation control motor - Mode control motor Does the voltage remain at about 5 V? Substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation. Replace the component that caused the voltage drop.

dtcs.description: or DTC indicator N and A/C ON: A Problem in the Blower Motor Circuit

steps.instruction: Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Do the Self-Diagnostic Function with the HDS   or the climate control unit.
Check for DTCs. Is DTC B1241 or N and A/C ON indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure. Check for loose wires or poor connections on the blower motor circuit.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Check the No. 8 (40 A) fuse in the under-hood fuse/relay box and the No. 16 (7.5 A) fuse in the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box. Are the fuses OK? Go to step 7. Replace the fuse(s), and recheck. If the fuse(s) blow again, check for a short in the No. 8 (40 A) fuse and/or the No. 16 (7.5 A) fuse circuit(s).
Disconnect the blower motor 2P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Measure the voltage between blower motor 2P connector terminal No. 1 and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 10. Go to step 29.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Reconnect the blower motor 2P connector.
Connect blower motor 2P connector terminal No. 2   to body ground with a jumper wire.

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). Does the blower motor run? Go to step 14. Replace the blower motor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Blower Motor/Service and Repair/Blower Unit Component Replacement.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the jumper wire.
Disconnect the power transistor 4P connector.

Check for continuity between power transistor 4P   connector terminal No. 2   and body ground. Is there continuity? Go to step 18. Check for an open in the BLK wire between the power transistor   and body ground. If the wire is OK, check for poor ground at G302.
Connect power transistor 4P connector terminals No. 2   and No. 4 with a jumper wire.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II). Does the blower motor run at high speed? Go to step 20. Repair open in the GRN wire between the power transistor   and the blower motor.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the jumper wire.
Disconnect climate control unit connector B (12P).

Check for continuity between body ground and climate control unit connector B (12P) terminals No. 5   and No. 6 individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to body ground in the wire(s)   between the climate control unit   and the power transistor. Go to step 24.

Check for continuity between the following terminals of climate control unit connector B (12P) and the power transistor 4P connector. Is there continuity? Go to step 25. Repair an open in the wire(s) between the climate control unit   and the power transistor.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between body ground and climate control unit connector B (12P) terminals No. 5   and No. 6 individually. Is there voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire(s) between the climate control unit   and the power transistor. Go to step 27.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Test the power transistor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Power Transistor HVAC/Testing and Inspection. Is the power transistor OK? Check for loose wires or poor connections at climate control unit   connector B (12P) and at the power transistor   4P connector. If the connections are good, substitute a known-good climate control unit, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original climate control unit  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Climate Control Unit Removal/Installation. Replace the power transistor.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Remove the blower motor relay from the under-hood fuse/relay box, and test it  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Relay/Testing and Inspection. Is the relay OK? Go to step 31. Replace the blower motor relay.

Check for continuity between blower motor relay 4P   socket terminal No. 2   and blower motor 2P connector terminal No. 1. Is there continuity? Go to step 32. Repair an open in the wire between the blower motor relay   and the blower motor.

Measure the voltage between blower motor relay 4P   socket terminal No. 1   and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 33. Replace the under-hood fuse/relay box  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Under-Hood Fuse/Relay Box Removal and Installation.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between blower motor relay 4P   socket terminal No. 4 and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Check for an open in the wire between the blower motor relay   4P socket terminal No. 3 and body ground. If the wire is OK, check for poor ground at G301. Repair an open in the wire between the No. 16 (7.5 A) fuse in the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box   and the blower motor relay.

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Turn the combination light switch to the PARKING LIGHT, AUTO, and ON (low beam) positions, and then to the OFF position.
Wait for at least 6 seconds.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Are DTCs B1275, B1276, B1277, and/or B1278 indicated? Go to step 6. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Select LIGHTING from the BODY ELECTRICAL SYSTEM SELECT menu, and enter the DATA LIST.

Check each combination light switch position value with the DATA LIST menu. When the combination light switch is turned OFF When the combination light switch is turned to PARKING LIGHT When the combination light switch is turned to AUTO When the combination light switch is turned ON (HEADLIGHT) Are all data list values correct? Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box, USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (USA Models), Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (Canada Models). Go to step 8.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the combination light switch 12P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Select LIGHTING from the BODY ELECTRICAL SYSTEM SELECT menu, and enter the DATA LIST.
Check each combination light switch position value with the DATA LIST menu. Are all data list values indicated OFF? Go to step 16. Go to step 13.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector R (24P).

Check for continuity between body ground and driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector R (24P)   terminals No. 6,No. 7,   and No. 8 individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire(s). Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box, USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (USA Models), Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (Canada Models).
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Do the combination light switch test  See: Lighting and Horns/Sensors and Switches - Lighting and Horns/Combination Switch/Service and Repair . Is the combination light switch OK? Go to step 18. Replace the combination light switch  See: Lighting and Horns/Sensors and Switches - Lighting and Horns/Combination Switch/Service and Repair.
Disconnect driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector R (24P).

Check for continuity between driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector R (24P) terminals and the combination light switch 12P connector terminals as shown: Is there continuity? Go to step 20. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire(s).

Check for continuity between driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector R (24P) terminals as shown: Is there continuity? Repair a short between the wires. Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box, USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (USA Models), Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (Canada Models).

Turn the combination light switch to the PARKING LIGHT, AUTO, and ON (low beam) positions, and then to the OFF position.
Wait for at least 6 seconds.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Are DTCs B1275, B1276, B1277, and/or B1278 indicated? Go to step 6. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Select LIGHTING from the BODY ELECTRICAL SYSTEM SELECT menu, and enter the DATA LIST.

Check each combination light switch position value with the DATA LIST menu. When the combination light switch is turned OFF When the combination light switch is turned to PARKING LIGHT When the combination light switch is turned to AUTO When the combination light switch is turned ON (HEADLIGHT) Are all data list values correct? Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box, USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (USA Models), Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (Canada Models). Go to step 8.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the combination light switch 12P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Select LIGHTING from the BODY ELECTRICAL SYSTEM SELECT menu, and enter the DATA LIST.
Check each combination light switch position value with the DATA LIST menu. Are all data list values indicated OFF? Go to step 16. Go to step 13.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector R (24P).

Check for continuity between body ground and driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector R (24P)   terminals No. 6,No. 7,   and No. 8 individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire(s). Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box, USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (USA Models), Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (Canada Models).
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Do the combination light switch test  See: Lighting and Horns/Sensors and Switches - Lighting and Horns/Combination Switch/Service and Repair . Is the combination light switch OK? Go to step 18. Replace the combination light switch  See: Lighting and Horns/Sensors and Switches - Lighting and Horns/Combination Switch/Service and Repair.
Disconnect driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector R (24P).

Check for continuity between driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector R (24P) terminals and the combination light switch 12P connector terminals as shown: Is there continuity? Go to step 20. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire(s).

Check for continuity between driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector R (24P) terminals as shown: Is there continuity? Repair a short between the wires. Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box, USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (USA Models), Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (Canada Models).

dtcs.description: Turn Signal Switch Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Operate the turn signal switch in the left and the right positions, and wait for at least 6 seconds.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1280 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Select LIGHTING from the BODY ELECTRICAL SYSTEM SELECT menu, then enter the DATA LIST.

Check each turn signal switch position value with the DATA LIST menu. When the turn signal switch is in left position When the turn signal switch is in right position Are all data list values correct? Faulty driver's MICU; replace the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box, USA models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (USA Models), Canada models  See: Maintenance/Fuses and Circuit Breakers/Fuse Block/Service and Repair/Driver's Under-Dash Fuse/Relay Box (Driver's MICU) Removal and Installation (Canada Models). Go to step 7.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the combination light switch 12P connector.
Disconnect driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector R (24P).

Check for continuity between body ground and driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector R (24P)   terminals No. 13   and No. 14   individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire(s). Go to step 11.

Check for continuity between driver's under-dash fuse/relay box connector R (24P) terminals No. 13   and No. 14. Is there continuity? Repair a short between the wires. Replace the combination light switch  See: Lighting and Horns/Sensors and Switches - Lighting and Horns/Combination Switch/Service and Repair.

dtcs.description: Auto Lighting Sensor Circuit Malfunction

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Wait for 1 second.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1575 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the automatic lighting sensor/sunlight sensor 5P   connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between body ground and automatic lighting sensor/sunlight sensor 5P connector terminal No. 1. Is there about 5 V? Replace the automatic lighting sensor/sunlight sensor  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Sensors and Switches - HVAC/Solar Sensor/Service and Repair. Go to step 9.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box connector D (28P).

Check for continuity between passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box connector D (28P) terminal No. 9   and automatic lighting sensor/sunlight sensor 5P connector terminal No. 1. Is there continuity? Faulty passenger's MICU; replace the passenger's under-dash fuse/relay box. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire. With Navigation B1775 DTC B1775: Microphone Input/Output Shorted to Power or Open (with navigation)
Connect the HDS to the DLC.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II).
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1775 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the front HFL-navigation-ANC/active sound control microphone 7P connector.
Disconnect the HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminal No. 13 and body ground. Is there voltage? There is a short to power in the wire between the HandsFreeLink control unit   and the front HFL-navigation-ANC/active sound control microphone. Replace the affected shielded harness. Go to step 10.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Check for continuity between HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminal No. 13 and front HFL-navigation-ANC/active sound control microphone 7P connector terminal No. 3. Is there continuity? Go to step 12. There is an open in the wire between the HandsFreeLink control unit   and the front HFL-navigation-ANC/active sound control microphone. Replace the affected shielded harness.
Disconnect audio-navigation unit connector H (28P).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminal No. 27 and body ground. Is there voltage? There is a short to power in the wire between the HandsFreeLink control unit   and the audio-navigation unit. Replace the affected shielded harness. Go to step 15.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Reconnect the HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector and the front HFL-navigation-ANC/active sound control microphone 7P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Do the HFL Self-Diagnostic Function. Do you hear, "The hands free system is OK"? Substitute a known-good audio-navigation unit (including the HDD)  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Service and Repair/Audio-Navigation Unit Removal/Installation, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original audio-navigation unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Service and Repair/Audio-Navigation Unit Removal/Installation. Substitute a known-good front HFL-navigation-ANC/active sound control microphone  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation System/Voice Activation Microphone/Service and Repair/Front HFL-Navigation-ANC/Active Sound Control Microphone Removal / Installation, and recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away, replace the original front HFL-navigation-ANC/active sound control microphone  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation System/Voice Activation Microphone/Service and Repair/Front HFL-Navigation-ANC/Active Sound Control Microphone Removal / Installation. If the symptom/indication is still present, replace the HandsFreeLink control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Relays and Modules - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation Module/Service and Repair. Without Navigation B1775 DTC B1775: Microphone Input/Output Shorted to Power or Open (without navigation)
Connect the HDS to the DLC.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.

Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminal No. 13 and body ground. Is there voltage? There is a short to power in the wire between the HandsFreeLink control unit   and the HFL microphone. Replace the affected shielded harness. Go to step 10.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Check for continuity between HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminals No. 13 and No. 14 and HFL microphone 3P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 2 respectively. Is there continuity? If the symptom/indication is still present, replace the HandsFreeLink control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Relays and Modules - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation Module/Service and Repair. There is an open in the wire(s) between the HandsFreeLink control unit   and the HFL microphone. Replace the affected shielded harness. Connect the HDS to the DLC.

If the symptom/indication is still present, replace the HandsFreeLink control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Relays and Modules - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation Module/Service and Repair. Connect the HDS to the DLC.

Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II).

Check for continuity between HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminal No. 13 and body ground, then between terminals No. 12 and No. 13. Is there continuity? dtcs.description: HandsFreeLink Steering Wheel Switch Failure

steps.instruction: Connect the HDS to the DLC.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), then start the vehicle and turn the steering wheel back and forth several times.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1779 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Do the HFL Switch Test  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Sensors and Switches - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation Switch/Testing and Inspection/HFL Switch Test. Is the switch OK? Go to step 7. Replace the HFL switch  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Sensors and Switches - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation Switch/Service and Repair/HFL Switch Replacement.
Disconnect the HandsFreeLink control unit 28P   connector.
Disconnect the HFL switch 7P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Measure the voltage between HFL switch 7P connector terminal No. 4 and body ground. Is there voltage? Repair a short to power on the BLU wire. Go to step 11.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Check for continuity between HandsFreeLink control unit 28P connector terminal No. 2 and HFL switch 7P connector terminal No. 4. Is there continuity? Go to step 13. Repair an open in the wire between the switch, the cable reel, and the HandsFreeLink control unit.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Measure the voltage between HandsFreeLink   control unit 28P connector terminal No. 2 and body ground. Is there voltage? Repair a short to power on the PUR wire. Go to step 15.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Check for continuity between HFL switch 7P connector terminal No. 5 and body ground. Is there continuity? Replace the HandsFreeLink control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Relays and Modules - Accessories and Optional Equipment/Voice Activation Module/Service and Repair. Repair an open in the wire between the switch, the cable reel, and the audio unit.

dtcs.description: Slide Pulse Error (No Change in Pulse for 1 Second)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Operate the power seat adjustment switch for several seconds in each direction.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1825 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1836 also indicated? Go to the DTC B1836 troubleshooting  See: B1836. Go to step 6.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Are DTCs B1826, B1827, and/or B1828 also indicated? Go to step 17. Go to step 7.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Operate the slide switch for 2 seconds, and check the slide motor operation. Does the slide motor run smoothly? Go to step 9. Go to step 24.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect power seat control unit connector A (40P).
Disconnect the slide motor/position sensor 5P connector.

Check for continuity between power seat control unit connector A (40P) terminals No. 29 and No. 31 and slide motor/position sensor 5P connector terminals No. 3 and No. 4 respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 13. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire(s).
Check for continuity between body ground and power seat control unit connector A (40P) terminal No. 31. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 14.
Reconnect power seat control unit connector A (40P).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between slide motor/position sensor 5P connector terminals No. 4 (+) and No. 3 (-). Is there about 5 V? Replace the slide motor/position sensor  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Motor/Service and Repair/Front Seat Slide Motor Replacement. If there is no voltage, replace the power seat control unit  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection. If there is battery   voltage, repair a short to power in the wire (sensor line).
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect power seat control unit connector A (40P).
Disconnect the slide motor/position sensor 5P connector.

Check for continuity between power seat control unit connector A (40P) terminals No. 15 and No. 29 and slide motor/position sensor 5P connector terminals No. 2 and No. 3 respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 21. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire(s).
Reconnect power seat control unit connector A (40P).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between power seat control unit connector A (40P) terminals No. 15 and No. 29. Is there battery voltage when the slide switch is operated? Replace the slide motor/position sensor  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Motor/Service and Repair/Front Seat Slide Motor Replacement. Replace the power seat control unit  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Check the No. 19 (20 A) fuse in the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK? Go to step 26. Replace the fuse. If the fuse blows again, repair a short to ground on the No. 19 (20 A) fuse circuit.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between body ground and power seat control unit connector B (18P) terminal No. 8. Is there less than 0.2 V? Go to step 28. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire between the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box   and the power seat control unit.

Measure the voltage between body ground and power seat control unit connector B (18P) terminal No.18, when the slide switch is operated. Is there less than 0.2 V? Go to step 29. Repair an open or high resistance in the ground wire or poor ground (G701).
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect power seat control unit connector B (18P).
Disconnect the slide motor/position sensor 5P connector.

Check for continuity between power seat control unit connector B (18P) terminals No. 7 and No. 17 and slide motor/position sensor 5P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 5 respectively. Is there continuity? Go to step 33. Repair an open or high resistance in the wire.

Check for continuity between body ground and slide motor/position sensor 5P connector terminals No. 1   and No. 5   individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire(s). Go to step 34.

Test the slide motor  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Motor/Testing and Inspection/Driver's Power Seat Motor Test. Is the motor OK? Replace the power seat control unit  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection. Replace the slide motor/position sensor  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Motor/Service and Repair/Front Seat Slide Motor Replacement.

Disconnect these connectors: - Slide motor/position sensor 5P connector - Front up-down motor/position sensor 5P connector - Rear up-down motor/position sensor 5P connector - Recline motor/position sensor 5P connector
Check for continuity between body ground and power seat control unit connector A (40P) terminal No. 15. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire. Go to step 8.

Check for continuity between body ground (motor body) and front up-down motor/position sensor, rear up-down motor/position sensor, and recline motor/position sensor connector terminal No. 3. Is there continuity? If the front or rear up-down motor/position sensor is faulty, replace the faulty front up-down motor/position sensor  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Motor/Service and Repair/Front Seat Up-Down Motor Replacement, rear up-down motor/position sensor. If the recline motor/position sensor is faulty, replace the recline motor/position sensor. Go to step 9.

Check for continuity between body ground (motor body) and slide motor/position sensor connector terminal No. 2. Is there continuity? Repair the slide motor/position sensor  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Motor/Service and Repair/Front Seat Slide Motor Replacement. Replace the power seat control unit  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection.

dtcs.description: Driving Position Memory Recall Time-out

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Move the power seat to the full front up and full rear up positions, then slide the seat all the way back and completely recline the seat-back backward with the power seat adjustment switch.
Set the seating position into memory by pressing either the Driver 1 or the Driver 2 position memory switch.
Using the power seat switches, move the seat away from the position just memorized.
Recall the memory position with the driving position memory switch.
Wait for 2 minutes.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Are any DTCs indicated? Go to step 9. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Are any DTCs other than B1837 indicated? Go to the indicated DTCs troubleshooting. Go to step 10.
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Move each power seat motor one at a time, and check the operation time. Does any motor fail to run smoothly? Go to the power seat motor test  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Motor/Testing and Inspection/Driver's Power Seat Motor Test. If the motor is OK, check the engagement of the motor gear. Go to the power seat control unit input test  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection.

Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and then back to ON (II).
Operate the slide switch in each direction for more than 1 second.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC B1844 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect power seat control unit connector A (40P).
Disconnect the power seat adjustment switch 12P connector.

Check for continuity between body ground and power seat control unit connector A (40P) terminals No. 1,No. 7,   and No. 24   individually. Is there continuity? Repair a short to ground in the wire(s). Go to step 9.

Check for continuity between these power seat control unit connector A (40P) terminals: - No. 1 and No. 7 - No. 1 and No. 24 - No. 7 and No. 24 Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wires. Go to step 10.

Test the power seat adjustment switch  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Switch/Service and Repair/Power Seat Adjustment Switch Test/Replacement (Driver's Side). Is the switch OK? Replace the power seat control unit  See: Body and Frame/Relays and Modules - Body and Frame/Power Seat Control Module/Testing and Inspection. Replace the power seat adjustment switch  See: Body and Frame/Seats/Power Seat Switch/Service and Repair/Power Seat Adjustment Switch Test/Replacement (Driver's Side).
